name: Peter's Cream of Pumpkin Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Pumpkin, fresh - cut into chunks
1 small garlic clove - peeled and crushed
1 1/2 tablespoons Low-fat white cheese (farmer's cheese, cream cheese or ricotta)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, heat the oil over low heat.
2. Add the vegetables (including fresh pumpkin but not canned), thyme, and bay leaf.
3. Reduce heat, cover, and let cook gently for 8 minutes. Stir occasionally to prevent browning.
4. Add broth and pumpkin (if using canned); bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes (fresh pumpkin) or 10 minutes (canned pumpkin).
5. Puree mixture in batches in a blender.
6. Pour pureed soup through a fine strainer back into saucepan and return to boil.
7. If too thick, thin with hot broth or water. (Can be made up to this point up to 2 days in advance and refrigerated, loosely covered.)
8. Before serving, reheat soup to boiling. Add cottage cheese and powdered milk; blend for 20 seconds.
9. Taste for seasoning. Ladle soup into 4 heated bowls; sprinkle with minced herbs. Serve immediately.
